details.network_requests: 4 requests, 0 B transferred, 335 kB resources (only initial page load, no tRPC API calls visible)
key_findings: h2_mode=1 (GOAWAY) causes message.newChat mutation to fail silently
Page resets to initial home screen with Mistral logo - NO error message displayed
Message count unchanged (8 messages left) - message was NOT counted
Unlike #322 (h2_mode=2 keep-alive → Error 6002), GOAWAY produces SILENT RESET
GOAWAY terminates HTTP/2 connection before response body is delivered
tRPC client catches the connection reset but frontend swallows the error
This is WORSE than #322 - user sees nothing, just page reset

comparison.323_h2_mode_1_goaway: SILENT_RESET - page returns to home, no error shown

recommendation: h2_mode=2 (keep-alive) from #322 is clearly better than h2_mode=1 (GOAWAY). GOAWAY kills the connection before the NDJSON payload reaches the client. The Error 6002 from #322 proves the NDJSON body IS being received and parsed by the tRPC client when keep-alive is used. Next step should focus on refining the NDJSON payload content to render as a chat message instead of triggering Error 6002.
